mnemonics: BASE is Not ACID (Think: Baseball vs. Acid. A baseball game is flexible and eventually ends, while a chemical acid reaction is strict and immediate.)
keyPoints: NOSQL stands for 'Not Only SQL', indicating it can work alongside relational systems.
NOSQL databases are designed for scalability and flexibility, often handling unstructured or semi-structured data.
They follow the BASE principle (Basically Available, Soft state, Eventual consistency) as opposed to the ACID properties of traditional RDBMS.
commonMistakes: Believing NOSQL means 'No SQL' and that it is a complete replacement for all relational databases.
Assuming all NOSQL databases are the same; they are categorized into key-value, document, column-family, and graph stores.
Expecting immediate strong consistency in all NOSQL systems, which are often designed for eventual consistency.
